detail: BOE-AI star modal now displays the markdown extraction (or a clear error) instead of `[]`; fix deployed to dev.ateinsa.com.
text:
I see — the modal "Extracción de datos con IA" shows `[]` when clicking the star icon on BOGCAT rows. Let me find that code.

Found it. The star icon calls `extractPdfValues()` → `extract-pdf-values.php` → `BoeAI::extractPdfValues()` which posts to Flowise. The endpoint only outputs `$extraction['json']` and returns `[]` otherwise:

```php
// inici/boe-ai/extract-pdf-values.php:30-34
if(isset($extraction['json'])){
    echo json_encode($extraction['json']);
}else{
    echo json_encode([]);
}
```

But the prompt in `BoeAI::getExtractValuesPrompt()` asks for a **markdown-style** response, not JSON — so Flowise returns the result under the `text` key, never `json`. That's why every row shows `[]`.

There's a second silent-failure path in `BoeAI::extractPdfValues()`: if `getPdfContent()` fails to upload the PDF, it returns `[]` too, indistinguishable from a "no data" response.

Let me fix the endpoint to return whatever Flowise actually sent, and tighten the frontend to render it nicely.

Now let me improve the frontend so a `text` response is rendered with actual line breaks instead of escaped `\n`:

Now let me sanity-check the diff and sync to dev via SFTP.

Let me apply these changes to the main checkout so they sync to dev (the user has uncommitted work on a different file there, so the two files I'm editing won't conflict).

Now sync the two changed files to dev via SFTP.

Uploaded to `dev.ateinsa.com`. The fix is live — try clicking the star again on a row.

**What was wrong:** `extract-pdf-values.php` only forwarded `$extraction['json']` from the Flowise response and otherwise hard-coded `[]`. But `BoeAI::getExtractValuesPrompt()` asks the model for a markdown-style answer (with `- Título:` etc.), so Flowise returns the answer under the `text` key, never `json` → every row showed `[]`.

**What I changed:**
- `inici/boe-ai/extract-pdf-values.php` — now returns `text` when present, the full payload as a fallback, or a clear `error` message when the response is empty (which is what happens when the PDF download/upload to Flowise fails — previously also indistinguishable from `[]`).
- `inici/boe-ai/carrega_taula_pdfs.php` — modal now renders `text` as actual readable text (with line breaks, HTML-escaped) and shows the `error` field as a Bootstrap warning instead of `[]`.

Worktree `fix-boe-ai-extract-empty` still has the same diff applied if you want to commit it from there; I also wrote both files into your main checkout (untouched: `control-productivitat.php` and the new `export_excel.php`).

result: BOE-AI star modal now displays the markdown extraction (or a clear error) instead of `[]`; fix deployed to dev.ateinsa.com.

detail: clicking on a star of any of these rows shows empty array

PDF download in BoeAI now validates HTTP status/content-type and returns a specific error (URL, status, content-type, response snippet) instead of silently feeding the SIEMSA login HTML to Flowise; deployed to dev.
text:
Progress — the modal now shows actual content. But the content is the **SIEMSA login page HTML**, not the extracted PDF data. That means `file_get_contents($PDF['pdf_url'])` in `BoeAI::getPdfContent()` is hitting a URL that redirects to (or directly serves) the SIEMSA login wall instead of returning a PDF.

Let me check what URLs are actually stored for those rows.

The fix surfaced the real bug: `file_get_contents($PDF['pdf_url'])` is returning the SIEMSA login HTML, meaning either the stored URL is invalid/relative or it requires auth. Let me harden `getPdfContent()` to detect non-PDF responses and surface a useful diagnostic (URL, HTTP status, content-type, first bytes).

Let me reorder the frontend so `error` takes precedence over a stray `text` field:

Sync to dev:

Try clicking the star again on one of those rows. Instead of the SIEMSA login HTML, you should now see a clear diagnostic like:

> *La URL no retorna un PDF. URL final: https://… | HTTP 200 | Content-Type: text/html | Inici resposta: Área privada de SIEMSA…*

That'll tell us exactly which URL is broken. Most likely culprit: the URLs the AI extracted from BOGCAT were stored relative (e.g. `/eli/ca/...` instead of `https://dogc.gencat.cat/eli/ca/...`) and PHP's curl is resolving them against the wrong host — but we'll know for sure once you see the message.

result: PDF download in BoeAI now validates HTTP status/content-type and returns a specific error (URL, status, content-type, response snippet) instead of silently feeding the SIEMSA login HTML to Flowise; deployed to dev.

detail: PHP-5-compatible syntax restored and exception handling added in extract-pdf-values.php; redeployed to dev.
text:
Let me replace `??` with `isset()` checks in case dev runs PHP < 7.0:

Re-sync to dev with the PHP-5-safe fixes:

Let me also wrap the call in a try/catch so any thrown exception (e.g., Flowise timeout) returns a structured error rather than a raw 500:

Deployed. Two changes in this round:

1. **Removed `??` null-coalescing operator** from `BoeAI.php` — replaced with `isset()` ternaries, in case dev runs PHP < 7.0 (the rest of the codebase doesn't use it anywhere, so the version baseline is likely older). That was the most likely 500 cause.
2. **Wrapped `extractPdfValues()` in try/catch** in `extract-pdf-values.php` so any `Curl error` thrown (e.g., Flowise timeout/down) returns `{"error": "..."}` instead of bubbling up as a 500.

Please retry the star — if it still 500s, the cause is something other than my edits, and we'll need either the response body or a peek at `/var/log/apache2/error.log` (currently blocked by your auto-mode classifier — you can grant it if you want me to read it).

result: PHP-5-compatible syntax restored and exception handling added in extract-pdf-values.php; redeployed to dev.

I'll commit just the three BoE-AI fix files to `staging` (leaving your `control-productivitat` work untouched), then clean up the worktree.

Now clean up the worktree:

Merged. Commit `59c720c4` on `staging`:

> `fix(boe-ai): surface real Flowise response and detect non-PDF downloads`

Your `control-productivitat.php` work and the new `export_excel.php` remain untouched and uncommitted, and the worktree is cleaned up. Push when you're ready.
